question_frequency.rationale: The Pair never asks the user a single question. It immediately delivers a fully-worked solution with no didactic engagement, no fork, no 'which would you pick' moment — despite the project being explicitly about two-option choices.

question_quality.rationale: There are no questions or option forks at all. The closed-form vs. iterate trade-off is mentioned only post-hoc in the user's recap, never surfaced as a choice.

scaffolding.rationale: No scaffolding exists — the answer is dumped in one shot with the boxed result. The user has no opportunity to reason or progress through subproblems.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and the math is well-presented, but it is a monologue, not a back-and-forth. The user simply restates the answer; there is no genuine conversational exchange.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
